items.example: The first item on the agenda is the budget report.
Please submit the invoice to the accounting department.
We need to negotiate the terms of the contract.
The company allocated additional resources to the project.
The new policy will be implemented next quarter.
The proposal was approved and subsequently put into effect.
The results demonstrate a significant improvement.
All employees must comply with safety regulations.
The board endorsed the new strategic plan.
We need to determine if the project is feasible.
We met with several prospective clients last week.
Please include your remittance with the order form.
The company plans to consolidate its operations.
Employees accumulate vacation days throughout the year.
Please specify your preferred delivery date.

items.title: 现在完成时
items.explanation: have/has + 过去分词，表示过去发生的动作对现在造成的影响，或从过去持续到现在的动作。常与 already, yet, just, since, for 连用。
examples: I have already submitted the report.
She has worked here for five years.
Have you finished the proposal yet?

items.title: 被动语态
items.explanation: be + 过去分词。在商务英语中常用被动语态以显得更正式、客观，也用于强调动作本身而非执行者。
examples: The meeting was postponed to next week.
All applications must be submitted by Friday.
The report is being prepared by the marketing team.

items.title: 词性转换
items.explanation: TOEIC Part 5 高频考点。掌握名词、动词、形容词、副词之间的转换规律。如：succeed (v) → success (n) → successful (adj) → successfully (adv)。
examples: The project was completed successfully.
She is a successful businesswoman.
His success inspired the team.

items.title: 主谓一致
items.explanation: 谓语动词必须与主语在人称和数上保持一致。注意集合名词（staff, team, committee）作整体看时用单数；each, every, one of 后接单数动词。
examples: The committee has reached a decision.
Each of the employees receives a bonus.
The number of applicants has increased.

items.title: 介词搭配
items.explanation: TOEIC 高频考点。注意固定的介词搭配：responsible for, capable of, interested in, familiar with, concerned about, dependent on。
examples: She is responsible for the marketing campaign.
He is capable of managing large teams.
We are familiar with the latest technology.

items.title: 条件句
items.explanation: Type 1: If + 现在时, will + 动词原形（可能发生）。Type 2: If + 过去时, would + 动词原形（不太可能）。Type 3: If + 过去完成时, would have + 过去分词（不可能发生）。
examples: If we increase the budget, we will finish faster.
If I were the manager, I would approve the plan.
If we had known earlier, we would have acted differently.

items.title: 动名词 vs 不定式
items.explanation: 某些动词后只能接动名词（enjoy, avoid, consider, finish, suggest），某些只能接不定式（decide, hope, plan, promise, want），有些两者皆可（begin, start, like, continue）。
examples: I enjoy working with international clients.
We plan to expand into Asian markets.
They started developing the new software.

items.title: 比较级与最高级
items.explanation: 短词加 -er/-est（fast→faster→fastest）；长词前加 more/most（efficient→more efficient→most efficient）。注意不规则变化：good→better→best, bad→worse→worst。
examples: This quarter's sales are higher than last quarter's.
She is the most qualified candidate for the position.
The new system is more efficient than the old one.

items.title: 定语从句
items.explanation: 关系代词 who（人，主格）, whom（人，宾格）, which（物）, that（人或物）, whose（所属关系）。非限定性定语从句（有逗号）只能用 who/which，不能用 that。
examples: The manager who hired me is retiring.
The report that I submitted was approved.
Ms. Lee, whose team won the award, will speak tomorrow.

items.title: 时态：将来时表达
items.explanation: will + 动词原形（即时决定/预测）；be going to + 动词原形（计划/打算）；现在进行时（已安排）；be to + 动词原形（正式安排/命令）。
examples: The conference will take place in March.
We are going to launch the product next month.
The CEO is to address the board on Friday.

items.usage: I'd like to follow up on our previous discussion about the contract.
She is in charge of the marketing department.
We will look into the matter and get back to you.
We must take into account the current market conditions.
I am writing on behalf of the entire team.
I'd like to bring up an important issue at the meeting.
The research team will carry out the survey next week.
We need to come up with a solution by Friday.
The legal team will draw up the contract.
I'll get back to you with the details by end of day.
Please keep track of all expenses during the trip.
I'd like to point out that the deadline is approaching.
The meeting has been put off until next Monday.
We are running out of time to complete the project.
The company set up a new branch in Singapore.
